---
title: "Interrail trip February"
description: "Map of my train trips last February using an interrail pass"
date: "2026-03-05"
image: "../images/trains-feb.jpg"
categories: [R, maps, trains]
toc: true
---

![](../images/trains-feb.jpg){.project-hero-img fig-alt="Map of my train trips last February using an interrail pass"}

::: {.project-meta}
::: {.meta-item}
📅 March 2026
:::
::: {.meta-item}
🛠️ R, map, trains
:::
::: {.meta-item}
📊 [Data source](OSM)
<!-- ::: -->
<!-- ::: {.meta-item} -->
<!-- 💻 [Code on GitHub](https://github.com/yourusername/repo) -->
:::
:::

## What & Why

I love traveling through Europe by train and every time I do I share on my social
networks the maps of the trips I make. Initially I shared relatively simple maps
made by the Rail Planner app but they didn't look accurate enough. That took me down
a rabbit hole I'm still in.

## Approach

I get the railway data from OSM, then look up the station coordinates using the DB API
and use dodgr to calculate the shortest path from station to station along the railways
This I lay over a political map of Europe. Then I use elevatr to get the elevation of 
each of the different pixels which I use for coloring the map.
